title: Reusable Client Architecture, Transport Abstractions, Middleware, and Configuration · Go Client Systems · Go Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A release board evaluating a changed infrastructure-provider contract must defend independent decisions across fresh cases, including one ambiguous failure and one changed boundary for reusable client architecture, transport abstractions, middleware, and configuration, producing small composable client library boundary. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a Go 1.26 net/http small composable client library boundary. Learners progress through prediction, explicit t…
objectives: Centralize base authority, transport, timeout, user agent, credentials, limits, and defaults in an explicit client configuration. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Inject the narrow request execution capability needed for deterministic tests without cloning the whole HTTP library. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Compose authentication, retries, metrics, tracing, caching, and redaction with documented ordering and ownership. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go Client Configuration · changed case · Quiz Client Configuration
steps.instruction:
quiz case — Go vendor-release candidate library needs base authority, injected transport, auth, retry, cache, metrics, redaction, and typed operation G-2. Order wrappers, separate per-request state, validate configuration, and keep protocol plumbing private. Competency probe: Centralize base authority, transport, timeout, user agent, credentials, limits, and defaults in an explicit client configuration. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
